edges.description: Reduced CTCF dosage weakens TAD boundaries and long-range insulator function.
Aberrant enhancer-promoter contacts misregulate developmental transcriptional programs.
Misexpression of cortical developmental genes alters progenitor maintenance and neuronal differentiation.
Persistent dysregulation of neurocognitive gene networks impairs intellectual development.
Widespread transcriptional defects delay acquisition of developmental milestones.
Altered cortical circuit maturation contributes to behavioral dysregulation.
Dysregulated neuronal excitability programs increase seizure susceptibility.
Craniofacial developmental gene dysregulation contributes to characteristic facial features.
Growth regulatory transcriptional disruption contributes to reduced linear growth.
Misregulated cardiac developmental transcription factors increase structural heart defect risk.
Perturbed craniofacial patterning pathways predispose to palatal fusion defects.
Developmental dysregulation of auditory pathway genes increases risk of conductive and/or sensorineural hearing impairment.
Premature progenitor exhaustion reduces final brain growth and head circumference.
Disrupted corticospinal and motor network maturation contributes to low muscle tone.
Neurodevelopmental impairment of oral motor coordination and autonomic regulation impairs feeding.

nodes.description: CTCF mutations induce premature depletion of stem-like neural progenitor cells and accelerate maturation of GABAergic neurons within the developing cortex. The R567W mutation causes reduced proportions of neural progenitors and radial glial cells, with increased postmitotic neuroblasts and inhibitory GABAergic neurons, disrupting the balanced development of excitatory and inhibitory neural circuits.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF mutation at R567 causes developmental disorders via 3D genome rearrangement and abnormal neurodevelopment.
evidence.snippet: This mutation induces premature stem-like cell exhaustion, accelerates the maturation of GABAergic neurons, and disrupts neurodevelopmental and synaptic pathways.

nodes.description: Behavioral issues are common and include autistic features, attention deficit and hyperactivity disorder (ADHD)-like features, aggression, and other behavioral disturbances.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Behavioral anomalies such as autistic features, attention deficit and hyperactivity or aggressivity were common and reported in 24 individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Expansion of the genotypic and phenotypic spectrum of CTCF-related disorder guides clinical management: 43 new subjects and a comprehensive literature review.
evidence.snippet: behavioral problems (52%).

nodes.description: Heterozygous loss-of-function mutations in CTCF lead to reduced functional CTCF protein levels, disrupting its role as a master regulator of chromatin architecture. CTCF is essential for establishing insulator elements that define topologically associating domain (TAD) boundaries. Both frameshift and missense variants (affecting zinc finger domains) result in functional haploinsufficiency through nonsense-mediated decay or impaired DNA binding.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in the genome organizer CTCF cause intellectual disability.
evidence.snippet: Our findings indicate that haploinsufficiency of CTCF affects genomic interaction of enhancers and their regulated gene promoters that drive developmental processes and cognition.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Our extensive follow-up study does not indicate any clear correlation between nature and location of variants and clinical presentation. Furthermore, differentially expressed genes significantly overlapped between individuals with either missense or LGD variants

nodes.description: Reduced CTCF function leads to aberrant chromatin looping and disrupted TAD boundaries, causing inappropriate enhancer-promoter interactions. CTCF normally mediates formation of chromatin loops that define TADs, and its depletion causes blurred domain boundaries and altered three-dimensional genome structure. The R567W mutation specifically affects CTCF binding to peripheral motifs, causing local chromatin structure alterations particularly at the clustered protocadherin locus.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF mutation at R567 causes developmental disorders via 3D genome rearrangement and abnormal neurodevelopment.
evidence.snippet: it specifically hinders CTCF binding to peripheral motifs upstream to the core consensus site, causing alterations in local chromatin structure and gene expression, particularly at the clustered protocadherin locus

nodes.description: Palatal anomalies including cleft palate or high palate are present in approximately one-third of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: palatal anomalies such as cleft palate or high palate were present in 12 individuals

nodes.description: Cardiac defects occur in a subset of affected individuals, including various structural heart abnormalities; GeneReviews also notes aortic ectasia among reported congenital heart defects.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Cardiac defects occurred in 11 individuals

evidence.reference_title: CTCF-Related Disorder.
evidence.snippet: gastrointestinal malrotation, genitourinary anomalies, and congenital heart defects, including aortic ectasia).

nodes.description: Minor facial dysmorphisms are frequently noted but do not constitute a recognizable facial gestalt. Features may include long face, prominent forehead, bulbous nasal tip, and long palpebral fissures.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Minor facial dysmorphisms were frequently noted but did not point to a recognizable, typical facial gestalt

nodes.description: Feeding problems in infancy, including poor feeding and failure to thrive, are among the most common features. Many affected individuals require temporary tube feeding.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: failure to thrive or feeding difficulties, often requiring tube feeding, occurred in 23 individuals

evidence.reference_title: Expansion of the genotypic and phenotypic spectrum of CTCF-related disorder guides clinical management: 43 new subjects and a comprehensive literature review.
evidence.snippet: feeding difficulties/failure to thrive (66%)

nodes.description: Delayed achievement of developmental milestones including motor and language domains. Walking age ranges from 12 months to over 3 years, and age of first words ranges from 12 months to absent speech at 12 years.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Walking age ranged from 12 months to three years, and age of first words from 12–18 months to lack of speech at age 12 years.

nodes.description: Conductive and/or sensorineural hearing loss occurs in approximately one-quarter of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Conductive and/or sensorineuronal hearing loss were reported in 10

nodes.description: Muscular hypotonia is commonly observed, contributing to motor developmental delays and feeding difficulties.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: muscular hypotonia were noted

nodes.description: Intellectual disability of variable severity is a core feature, ranging from learning difficulties and normal IQ to severe intellectual disability. Some individuals attend mainstream school or graduate from college, while others require substantial lifelong support.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Cognitive impairment was extremely variable, ranging from learning difficulties, normal IQ, and attending mainstream school or graduating from college in seven individuals to severe ID in three individuals.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in the genome organizer CTCF cause intellectual disability.
evidence.snippet: we now identified two de novo frameshift mutations and one de novo missense mutation in CTCF in individuals with intellectual disability, microcephaly, and growth retardation

evidence.reference_title: Expansion of the genotypic and phenotypic spectrum of CTCF-related disorder guides clinical management: 43 new subjects and a comprehensive literature review.
evidence.snippet: The cardinal clinical features in subjects with CRD included intellectual disability/developmental delay (91%) with speech delay (65%), motor delay (53%), feeding difficulties/failure to thrive (66%), ocular abnormalities (56%), musculoskeletal anomalies (53%), and behavioral problems (52%).

nodes.description: Microcephaly or borderline microcephaly is present in approximately 30-50% of affected individuals, reflecting impaired neurogenesis and brain development.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: (borderline) microcephaly in 12 individuals

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in the genome organizer CTCF cause intellectual disability.
evidence.snippet: we now identified two de novo frameshift mutations and one de novo missense mutation in CTCF in individuals with intellectual disability, microcephaly, and growth retardation

nodes.description: CTCF haploinsufficiency leads to widespread gene dysregulation affecting neurodevelopmental pathways. RNA-sequencing in affected individuals revealed 3,828 deregulated genes enriched for known neurodevelopmental disorder genes and transcriptional regulation processes. Downregulated genes are enriched for transcription-related processes, while upregulated genes are enriched for translational and ribosomal processes.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: RNA-sequencing in five individuals identified 3828 deregulated genes enriched for known NDD genes and biological processes such as transcriptional regulation

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in the genome organizer CTCF cause intellectual disability.
evidence.snippet: Transcriptome analyses in all three individuals with point mutations revealed deregulation of genes involved in signal transduction and emphasized the role of CTCF in enhancer-driven expression of genes.

nodes.description: Febrile or nonfebrile seizures occur in a minority of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Febrile or nonfebrile seizures or teeth anomalies occurred in four individuals

nodes.description: Postnatal growth restriction and short stature are observed in a subset of affected individuals. Intrauterine growth restriction may also occur.

evidence.reference_title: CTCF variants in 39 individuals with a variable neurodevelopmental disorder broaden the mutational and clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Postnatal short stature was noted in 6

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in the genome organizer CTCF cause intellectual disability.
evidence.snippet: individuals with intellectual disability, microcephaly, and growth retardation
